Agent1 Round 1: Tissue conditioners, while useful initially after placement or during healing phases, can become problematic if left too long due to their properties. They tend to harden over time which could affect surrounding tissue health and function.

My answer is A: Harden in mouth. The hardening property of tissue conditioners makes them unsuitable for prolonged use within the oral cavity as it may lead to adverse effects on adjacent tissues. Your analysis correctly identifies that one primary limitation of using tissue conditioners is their tendency to harden when exposed to saliva or other conditions inside the mouth. Answer: A
